backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor that combines blob storage and authorization mixins to implement user profile management, event/announcement CRUD, and a contact form with role-based access control.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report confirming that the Hunting activity card was added to RecreationPage.tsx with high confidence, mapped to test ID REQ-10.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries for each frontend source file, serving as a reference index for the project's frontend structure.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS entry file that defines OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark themes, covering background, foreground, card, chart, and sidebar color tokens.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML shell for the Vite/React app, mounting the React root div and loading the main.tsx module entry point.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that configures TanStack Router with a shared Layout (Navigation + Footer) and defines routes for all six pages: Home, About, Services, Events, Recreation, and Contact.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Site footer component rendering brand info, quick navigation links, contact details, copyright year, and a caffeine.ai attribution link with UTM parameters.
frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx: Sticky top navigation bar with a logo, desktop nav links with active-state highlighting, mobile hamburger menu, and an Internet Identity-based Admin Login/Logout button.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom React hook that creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous ICP backend actor using TanStack Query, reinitializing and invalidating dependent queries whenever the Internet Identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication on the ICP platform, managing login/logout state, identity persistence, and AuthClient configuration.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching and mutating backend data including events, town contact info, messages, and user profile/admin status.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet importing Google Fonts and defining Tailwind CSS base styles with a Northern Maine wilderness-themed color palette for light and dark modes.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that renders the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/AboutPage.tsx: Static page displaying information about Jackman, Maine, including quick facts (location, population, elevation) and descriptive town history content.
frontend/src/pages/ContactPage.tsx: Contact page that fetches town address and phone number from the backend and provides a form for submitting messages via a mutation hook.
frontend/src/pages/EventsPage.tsx: Events page that lists town events fetched from the backend, with admin-only controls to add or delete events using mutation hooks.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: The main landing page for the Jackman, Maine town website, featuring a hero section with navigation CTAs, quick links to Events/Recreation/Services, and highlight cards showcasing outdoor activities.
frontend/src/pages/RecreationPage.tsx: A recreation activities page listing outdoor pursuits available in Jackman, Maine (snowmobiling, fishing, hiking, paddling, camping, wildlife photography, and hunting) with descriptions and highlights for each.
frontend/src/pages/ServicesPage.tsx: A town services page displaying emergency contacts (911), and municipal service details for the Town Office, Public Works, Solid Waste, Library, and Motor Vehicle Registration.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, storing, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routes, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom color palettes (forest, slate, amber), typography/container-queries/animate plugins, and custom serif/sans font families for the project.
project_state.json: Tracks the project state (version 7) for the Jackman, Maine town website, listing all existing features, requirements, architectural notes, and known issues.
